items.title: USDA updates guidance on SNAP food-restriction waivers and retailer compliance (page updated March 6, 2026)
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service updated its guidance page on SNAP food-restriction waivers and retailer compliance on 2026-03-06. The update signals ongoing federal oversight as states pursue waivers to restrict certain items; applicants should confirm their state’s approved waiver scope and retailer compliance requirements on USDA’s guidance portal.

items.title: USDA posts SNAP FY 2026 cost-of-living adjustments (COLA) tables
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service posted SNAP FY 2026 cost-of-living adjustment information on 2026-02-20. Households should check the FY 2026 COLA tables for updated maximum allotments, deductions, and income eligibility standards that affect benefit amounts.

items.title: USDA: SNAP online purchasing available in all 50 states and D.C. (territories still limited)
items.summary: USDA says SNAP online purchasing is available in all 50 states and the District of Columbia, with Guam and the U.S. Virgin Islands listed as not yet implemented. USDA also reminds participants that SNAP benefits can only be used for eligible food and cannot be used to pay delivery, service, or convenience fees.

items.title: KFF updates Medicaid enrollment and unwinding tracker (posted March 27, 2026)
items.summary: KFF’s Medicaid Enrollment and Unwinding Tracker notes that, in the archived unwinding data, at least 25,198,000 people were disenrolled during the continuous coverage unwinding as of 2024-09-12, with 56.4 million renewals reported in states that provided data. KFF also lists enrollment trend data through December 2025 and highlights that most states completed unwinding renewals by August 2024.

items.title: IRS: 2026 filing season opened Jan. 26; Where’s My Refund? updates Feb. 21 for early EITC/ACTC filers
items.summary: The IRS opened the 2026 tax filing season on 2026-01-26 and said Where’s My Refund? would show projected deposit dates for most early Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) and Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) filers by 2026-02-21. The IRS also said most EITC/ACTC refunds should be available by 2026-03-02 for taxpayers using direct deposit with no other issues, and noted the federal filing deadline is 2026-04-15.

items.title: Senators urge HHS to release remaining FY 2026 LIHEAP funds (about $400 million)
items.summary: A bipartisan group of 40 U.S. Senators urged HHS to immediately release the remaining roughly $400 million in FY 2026 LIHEAP funding, after about $3.7 billion had already been released. The release cites total FY 2026 LIHEAP funding of $4.045 billion (about a $20 million increase over the prior year).

items.title: FFIS: HHS releases initial FY 2026 LIHEAP funds (reported as $3.7 billion)
items.summary: Federal Funds Information for States (FFIS) reports that HHS released initial FY 2026 LIHEAP funding totaling $3.7 billion. Households should check their state LIHEAP administering agency for application timelines and benefit availability as states receive and distribute their allocations.

items.title: USDA: WIC state agencies must authorize at least one non-fresh fruit/vegetable option by April 2026; 95% already compliant
items.summary: USDA says recent WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one form of fruits and vegetables beyond fresh (frozen, canned, and/or dried) by April 2026. USDA also reports that, as of November 2025, 95% of WIC state agencies were already in compliance.

items.title: HUD: 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding signed Feb. 3; PHAs told to plan for cost-saving measures and spring HAP allocations
items.summary: HUD’s 2026 Budget Management Letter says the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2026 was signed into law on 2026-02-03, providing 2026 funding for the Housing Choice Voucher program. HUD says PHAs should expect their 2026 Housing Assistance Payments (HAP) funding amounts in the spring after reconciliation of 2025 Voucher Management System data, and notes an estimated HAP proration factor of 99% for planning.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: HUD extends HOTMA compliance date for multifamily owners to Jan. 1, 2027
items.summary: HUD Notice H 2025-07 extends the compliance date for multifamily owners to be in full compliance with the HOTMA final rule (and updated income/asset documentation requirements) to 2027-01-01. The notice revises earlier guidance that had set the compliance date as 2025-01-01 and then 2026-01-01.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: SSA: 2026 COLA is 2.8%; SSI federal payment standard rises to $994 (individual) and $1,491 (couple)
items.summary: SSA says Social Security and SSI recipients receive a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026, based on CPI-W changes through Q3 2025. SSA’s fact sheet lists the 2026 SSI federal payment standard at $994 per month for an individual (up from $967) and $1,491 per month for a couple (up from $1,450).

items.title: Federal Student Aid posts 2026–27 FAFSA deadlines (federal submission deadline June 30, 2027)
items.summary: Federal Student Aid lists the 2026–27 FAFSA form submission deadline as 11:59 p.m. Central time on 2027-06-30, with corrections/updates due by 11:59 p.m. Central time on 2027-09-12. The page also notes that states and colleges may have earlier deadlines and provides a state-by-state list.
